task_completion.description: Verify the NREL 5MW sample project is loaded in QBlade.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for QBlade's main window showing 'NREL 5MW' or similar in the project tree, loaded airfoils, or window title.

task_completion.description: Verify the HAWT Blade Design module is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the HAWT Blade Design interface in QBlade, which typically features a 3D view of the blade and a geometry table.

task_completion.description: Verify the NACA 4412 airfoil is used for the blade.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'NACA 4412' selected in the 'Foil' or 'Polar' column of the blade design table.

task_completion.description: Verify the blade has at least 5 stations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the rows in the blade geometry table; there must be at least 5 rows.

task_completion.description: Verify the blade length is approximately 10 meters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Pos' or 'Radius' column in the blade geometry table; the final row should have a value of approximately 10.

task_completion.description: Verify the project is saved as hawt_blade.wpa in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog showing the file name 'hawt_blade.wpa' in '/home/ga/Documents/projects/', or a file manager/terminal showing the file exists.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the QBlade GUI to design the blade.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the QBlade application, specifically the blade design table and 3D viewer.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply create a dummy file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of a text editor being used to write a file named 'hawt_blade.wpa'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires designing a HAWT blade in QBlade. The blade must use the NACA 4412 airfoil, have at least 5 stations, and be approximately 10 meters long. The final project must be saved as hawt_blade.wpa in /home/ga/Documents/projects/. The VLM should verify these parameters in the QBlade HAWT Blade Design module table.
